title: chamaecrista gilliesii
intro: Chamaecrista gilliesii, also known as the Queensland buttonweed or gillies' sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the bean family, Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. Chamaecrista gilliesii is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as creams and salves, to promote wound healing and skin health.
